articles.title: SUNLIT CANYON
articles.author: Ethel Jacobson
articles.text:
Dazzle lies
On leaf and stream—
A dancing brilliance,
A quicksilver gleam.
Wherever the live oak
Canopy parts,
The sun's massed archers
Loose their darts
To splinter on eddy,
Stone, and stem,
Making a shattered
Mirror of them,
Reflecting each other
Diamond-bright,
A fluid,
Shimmering bedight
Landscape less of land
Than light.

articles.title: WORDS
articles.author: Armoral Kent
articles.text:
Words are such hasty things,
Yet they may mean
All that can ever be,
All that has been.

Words are such little things,
Yet they may make
Destinies totter,
Centuries shake.

Words are so uniform,
Yet they may be
Anything, everything,
To you and me.
